name: Boxing
description: Boxing is a one-on-one combat sports game. Two boxers face off in a ring with the goal of knocking out their opponent or winning by decision after a set number of rounds. The game features realistic boxing mechanics, including different types of punches (jabs, hooks, uppercuts, body blows), blocking, dodging, footwork (moving around the ring), and stamina management. Players must strategically use their punches and defensive maneuvers to deplete their opponent's health and stamina, while conserving their own. A stamina bar will be a visible metric. A health bar will be another visible metric. Knockdowns will occur when a boxer's health reaches zero or a critical state. After a knockdown, the knocked-down boxer will have a brief period to get back to their feet before being counted out. The game includes a career mode where players can create their own boxer, train them, and compete in tournaments to climb the ranks and become a champion. There's also a quick match mode for immediate action against a computer-controlled opponent or another player. Different boxer archetypes should be available (e.g., puncher, counter-puncher, boxer-puncher), each with unique strengths and weaknesses in terms of speed, power, stamina, and defense. A scoring system, based on landed punches and aggression, will determine the winner if there is no knockout.
